a: An object used to identify a cellular call and determine its state.
An object that provides a list of current cellular calls, and provides the ability to respond to state changes for calls.
The call state when fully establishing the call for all parties involved.
The call state, before establishing the connection, when the user initiates the call.
The call state upon call termination.
The call state, before establishing the connection, when a call is incoming but not yet answered by the user.
Information about the user’s cellular service provider, such as its unique identifier and whether it allows VoIP calls on its network.
An object indicating whether the app can access cellular data.
The possible states of the cellular data policy.
A state that allows access to cellular data.
A state that denies access to cellular data.
A state whose access to cellular data is unknown.
The type of cellular plan available for an eSIM.

The cellular plan is available for data only.
An object you use for an eSIM.
A type that contains lifecycle details for a time-limited cellular plan.
An object you use to download and install a carrier eSIM.
The result from attempting to provision an eSIM.

An object that validates tokens for UPI device verification or checks the availability of cellular plans for a phone number.
Constants that indicate the authorization status for accessing cellular plan information for a phone number.

A status that indicates the person didn’t grant authorization, or explicitly denied it.
A status that indicates a feature is unavailable for the given phone number.

A status that indicates the phone number has an active cellular plan on the device.

A high level of confidence about the availability of a cellular plan.
A low level of confidence about the availability of a cellular plan.
A type representing a Core Telephony error.
